data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 5 AM ET
data.description:
Global Markets Mixed as Investors Look Toward U.S., China Talks

Stock markets globally were mixed with U.S. futures pretty much flat following Thursday's bounce after the U.S.-U.K. trade deal.

China's Exports to U.S. Plunge, in Sign of Bite From Trump Tariffs

The drop in U.S.-bound shipments was offset by a surge in Chinese goods to Southeast Asia, Africa, Europe and Latin America.

BOJ Rate Hike Unlikely Until Trade Uncertainty Clears

The Bank of Japan will likely hold fire for the next few months due to growing concerns about the impact of U.S. tariffs, a former member of the BOJ's policy board says.

Trump's Bespoke Trade Deal With U.K. Sets Little Precedent for Other Nations

Many other agreements weren't seen as likely to come together quickly.

With U.K. Deal, U.S. Signals That 10% Tariff on World Is New Baseline

Both countries are hailing this as a good outcome, showing how much the landscape has shifted.

Bitcoin Price, XRP Soar. Here's the Risk to the Crypto Rally.

While Bitcoin and other cryptos have seen impressive double digit gains this week there are risks to the rally.

RBA to Walk Rate-Cut Path With Dangers on Both Sides

The Reserve Bank of Australia is set to cut rates and keep on cutting until the official cash rate is at a far less restrictive level.

New York Fed Survey Finds Consumers Ambivalent on Inflation Outlook

Consumers sent mixed signals about their inflation expectations in the New York Fed's April survey. But the poll showed no serious rise in long-term inflation expectations.

Canada's Financial Stability Faces Stiff Test From U.S. Tariffs, Bank of Canada Says

Annual central bank report says there is a risk permanent tariffs could cause high unemployment and business insolvencies.

EU Targets American Aircraft, Car Parts for Possible Tariffs if Talks Fail

The bloc said it could target more than $100 billion worth of American products with tariffs if negotiations with the U.S. break down.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 5 AM ET
data.description:
Panasonic Holdings to Cut 10,000 Jobs Globally

The Japanese electronics maker said it would re-evaluate the numbers of organizations and personnel needed.

Commerzbank Posts Higher Earnings After Commission Income Lifts Revenue

Earnings and revenue for the first quarter exceeded analyst expectations, after higher commission income offset a decline in interest income.

IAG to Buy 53 Planes Despite Market Uncertainty

British Airways owner International Consolidated Airlines said it ordered the planes to bolster its long-haul fleet after earnings rose.

Coinbase Reports Drop in Profit

Earlier in the day Coinbase announced a $2.9 billion cash-and-stock deal to acquire Deribit, a bitcoin options exchange.

McKesson to Spin Off Medical-Surgical Company

The pharmaceutical distributor said the new company would be a differentiated medical surgical supply and solutions business.

Lyft Swings to Profit Amid Increase in Ridership

San Francisco-based company said rides grew 16% year over year, to 218.4 million, ahead of analyst views for 215.1 million.

CoStar Agrees to Buy Australian Advertiser Domain for $1.9 Billion

U.S. real-estate analytics provider CoStar agreed to buy property advertiser Domain in a sweetened deal.

News Corp Revenue Boosted by Gains at Dow Jones, HarperCollins

Digital and print Wall Street Journal subscriptions totaled 4.34 million for the March quarter, the company says.

Peloton Slashes Marketing Again in Hard Play for Profitability

Double-digit percentage cuts continue for the fourth consecutive quarter as the fitness company's marketing chief leaves.

TKO Group Raises Full-Year Outlook on Recent Acquisitions, Swings to Profit

The new forecasts came as TKO Group swung to a profit and posted higher-than-expected revenue in the recent quarter.

data.title: India's SJS Enterprises jumps after Q4 profit rise
data.description:
** Shares of SJS Enterprises rise 12% to 1,044.65 rupees

** Auto components maker's Q4 consol net profit rises 25.1% Y/Y; revenue up 7.3% Y/Y

** Q4 EBITDA margin at 26.1% vs 26.2% year ago

** Trading vols at 892,094 shares, nearly 10x the 30-day average

** SJSE down ~10% YTD

data.title: Citi Downgrades Anhui Heli to Sell from Neutral; Price Target is 15.20 Yuan
data.description: Anhui Heli has an average rating of buy and mean price target of 18.61 yuan, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Gourgen Traffic Construction's Consortium Bags 704 Million Yuan Land Development Project
data.description:
A consortium with Anhui Gourgen Traffic Construction won the bid for a construction project for about 704 million yuan, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

Under the project, the consortium will manage the residential and commercial development of two land parcels in Baohe District, Hefei City, Anhui Province, China.

The Chinese infrastructure construction services provider will undertake 50% of the construction work. The company's share in the contract amounted to 352 million yuan.

China Coal Third Construction, a consortium member, will manage the other 50% of the construction work. Another consortium member, Anhui Xiangyuan Planning and Design, will be responsible for the project design.

Gourgen Traffic Construction's shares closed over 3% higher.

data.title: India offers to slash tariff gap by two-thirds in dash to seal trade pact with Trump
data.description:
Key points:
  • India offers to narrow tariff gap with U.S. to under 4%
  • India wants exemption from all "current, potential" tariff hikes
  • India ready to give preferential access to 90% of U.S. goods
  • India wants U.S. to align it with allies on critical tech

India has offered to slash its tariff gap with the U.S. to less than 4% from nearly 13% now, in exchange for an exemption from President Donald Trump's "current and potential" tariff hikes, two sources said, as both nations move fast to clinch a deal.

This would mean that the average tariff differential between India and the U.S., calculated across all products without weighting for trade volume, would be reduced by 9 percentage points, in one of the most sweeping changes to bring down trade barriers in the world's fifth largest economy.

The United States is India's largest trading partner, with bilateral trade totalling some $129 billion in 2024. The trade balance is currently in favour of India, which runs a $45.7 billion surplus with the U.S.

Trump announced on Thursday his administration's first "breakthrough deal" with Britain. It lowers average British tariffs on U.S. goods but keeps in place the 10% base tariff imposed by Washington on British goods, likely setting a template for Washington's approach with other trading partners.

Last month, Trump announced a 90-day pause on his long-planned reciprocal tariffs on global trading partners, including a 26% tariff on India, while his administration negotiates trade deals. A 10% base tariff continues to apply to India and many other nations during the pause.

After the UK, India and Japan are the next two nations in line to finalise a deal, a third Indian government official said. "We will see which one crosses the line first."

To achieve this, New Delhi has offered to reduce duties to zero on 60% of the tariff lines in the first phase of the deal which is under negotiation, said the first two sources, both Indian government officials familiar with the matter.

India has offered preferential access to nearly 90% of goods imported from the United States, including the reduced tariffs, one of the two officials said.

Details of India's offer to slash the tariff gap and what it has asked the U.S. in return have not been previously reported.

A delegation of Indian officials is likely to visit the U.S. later this month to take the negotiations forward, a fourth official said, adding that India's trade minister, Piyush Goyal, might visit too but his plans were not finalised.

All four government officials did not wish to be identified as details of the negotiations are private and sensitive.

India's trade ministry, which is leading talks, did not respond to a request for comment.

PREFERENTIAL ACCESS

Alongside tariff exemptions, India has also asked for preferential market access for key export sectors including gems and jewellery, leather, apparel, textiles, plastics, chemicals, oilseeds, shrimp, and horticultural produce such as bananas and grapes.

"Preferential market access for India would mean better terms of trade for these goods compared to America's other trading partners," the first official said.

India is also looking for concessions that would give it an edge over competitors in supplying "products of interest", the official added.

However, India's expectation of being exempted completely from tariffs on its exports is at odds with the deal struck between the U.S. and Britain.

To make the deal more attractive for Washington, India has offered to ease export regulations on several high-value U.S. exports, the first official said.

These include aircraft and parts, luxury cars and electric vehicles, telecom equipment, medical devices, hydrocarbons, wines and whiskey, berries, prunes, certain chemicals, and animal feed.

Beyond tariffs, India has also asked the U.S. to treat it at par with other top U.S. allies such as Britain, Australia and Japan in critical technology sectors such as AI, telecoms, biotech, pharmaceuticals, and semiconductors.

Washington's desire to share critical technologies with allies like India has often faced hurdles due to the U.S. government's own restrictive rules.

data.title: Prices rangebound amid softer demand, warmer temperatures
data.description:
Dutch and British wholesale gas prices continued to trade in a narrow range amid softer demand and higher temperatures.

The benchmark Dutch front-month contract (TRNLTTFMc1) inched up 0.17 euro to 35.30 euros per megawatt hour (MWh), or $11.27/mmBtu, at 0900 GMT, while the July contract edged up by 0.52 euro to 35.67 euros/MWh, according to LSEG data.

The British June contract (TRGBNBPMc1) was down 0.57 pence at 84.96 p/therm and the day-ahead contract (TRGBNBPD1) was down by 1.55 pence at 81.10 p/therm.

Average temperatures in north-west Europe are expected to be 2 degrees Celsius higher on the day-ahead.

Local distribution zone demand, mainly used for heating, is forecast to be 335 gigawatt hours per day (GWh/d) lower at 1,126 GWh/d on the day-ahead, and slightly higher over the weekend and working days next week, LSEG data showed.

"Combined with bullish technical indicators, we expect TTF (day-ahead) to continue rising moderately," said LSEG analyst Dzmitry Dauhalevich.

Total Norwegian exports are nominated 30 million cubic meters per day (mcm) higher at 284 mcm/day.

The European Parliament voted on Thursday in favour of loosening the EU's rules on filling gas storage, agreeing with concerns from member states that the targets risk inflating energy prices.

In the European carbon market, the benchmark contract (CFI2Zc1) was up 0.30 euro at 71.03 euros per metric ton.

data.title: Macquarie Downgrades Cosmos Pharmaceutical to Underperform from Neutral; Price Target is 8,000 Yen
data.description: Cosmos Pharmaceutical has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of 9,216.15 yen, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Record backlogs signal defence industry fragility
data.description:
Shares in European weapons makers are soaring on hopes NATO countries will boost military spending to 3% of GDP. But a mushrooming order book highlights the difficulty firms like Rheinmetall may face in meeting demand. Governments will need to help the sector build more capacity.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @pierrebri on X

CONTEXT NEWS

Defence Minister Boris Pistorius wants to increase Germany’s military budget for 2025 by up to 10 billion euros, to 63 billion euros, sources told Reuters.

Pistorius, the only minister to keep his job in the new government of Chancellor Friedrich Merz, has long advocated a significant boost of the country’s defence effort.

The German parliament earlier this year changed its constitution to exempt military spending from the country’s strict budget rules.

According to NATO, Germany spent 2.1% of its GDP on defence in 2024.

Hensoldt, the German radar maker, said on May 7 that its order backlog rose to 6.9 billion euros in the last quarter, an increase of 18% compared to a year ago. The group said it anticipated revenue of 2.5 billion euros to 2.6 billion euros this financial year.

data.description: AIA Group has an average rating of buy and mean price target of HK$89.10, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Crude Oil Prices Climb Amid Looming U.S.-China Trade Talks, U.S.-U.K. Trade Deal
data.description:
Crude oil prices surged on Friday amid signs of easing trade tensions between the U.S. and China and after the announcement of a trade deal between the U.S. and the U.K.

Brent crude at last look rose 1.2% to US$63.58/barrel and West Texas Intermediate crude gained 1.3% to US$60.66/b.

U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and China's Vice Premier He Lifeng are set to meet on Saturday in Switzerland to discuss how to resolve their countries' trade disputes, which have fueled concerns about the oil demand outlook.

"If the two set a date to start formal trade negotiations and agree to ratchet down their current steep tariffs against each other while talks carry on, markets will get a breather and crude could stack on another $2-$3 per barrel," Reuters quoted Vandana Hari, founder of oil market analysis provider Vanda Insights, in a Friday report.

Meanwhile, U.S. President Donald Trump and U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced a deal in which the U.K. will lower tariffs on U.S. imports to 1.8% from 5.1%, while the U.S. will cut duties on U.K. cars but left a 10% tariff on most other goods, the report said.

However, the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries and allied producers' decision to continue boosting output is expected to continue putting pressure on oil prices, Reuters reported.

data.title: Japan's Leading, Coinicident Indicators Sluggish in March
data.description:
Posing a challenge for the Bank of Japan, the nation's index of coincident economic indicators fell 1.3% to 116.0 in March from 117.3 in February, striking the metric's lowest level since November, the Cabinet Office reported Friday.

The March index indicated an economy that was "halting to fall," said the the government agency.

The Japan coincident index measures factors including factory output, employment, exports and retail sales to gauge economic activity.

In addition, Japan's leading economic indicators index declined 0.5% to 107.7 in March from 108.2 in February, said the Cabinet Office.

The leading indicator measures the economic outlook for Japan based on factors such as job offers and consumer sentiment.

The possibly slowing Japan economy may pose a dilemma for the Bank of Japan.

At its policy meeting ended May 1, the Bank of Japan said the nation's "economic growth is likely to moderate, as trade and other policies in each jurisdiction lead to a slowdown in overseas economies and to a decline on domestic corporate profits and other factors."

Japan's central bank lowered its estimate for growth of the the nation's gross domestic product (GDP) to 0.5% for the fiscal year started April 1, from the previous 1.1% outlook.

At the May 1 meeting, the Bank of Japan also voted to hold its key policy interest rate at 0.5%, holding rates fixed for the second-straight policy session.

Yet Japan's inflation rate on the consumer price index-core (CPI-core) that strips out fresh foods, logged at 3.2% on year in March, striking above the annual 2% target of the Bank of Japan.

Next week, Japan officials will report on the nation's first quarter GDP, and the possibly negative results may raise further dilemmas for the Bank of Japan, said ING Think, an arm of the Dutch investment house on Friday.

"Japan's economy is expected to contract in the first quarter by 0.1% quarter on quarter, seasonally adjusted, following a 0.6% gain in the last three months of 2024," said ING Think.

data.title: Israel will not be involved in Gaza aid distribution, US ambassador Huckbee says
data.description:
Israel will not be involved in Gaza aid distribution but will take part in providing security, U.S. ambassador to Israel Mike Huckabee said in a press conference on Friday.

Israel has been imposing an aid blockade on the enclave since March. Huckabee said the supply of aid was not dependent on reaching a ceasefire.

data.title: Melco International Development Unit's Q1 Profit More Than Doubles
data.description:
Melco International Development subsidiary Melco Resorts & Entertainment's attributable profit more than doubled to $32.5 million in the first quarter from $15.2 million in the year-ago period, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Earnings per share at the resort operator increased to $0.026 from $0.012 in the prior-year period.

Total operating revenues grew 11% to $1.23 billion from $1.11 billion in the year-ago period.

data.description:

data.title: Bulgaria Consumer Morale Improves in Q2
data.description:
Bulgaria’s consumer confidence indicator rose to -16.4 in the second quarter of 2025 from an over one-year low of -19.5 in the previous three-month period.

Consumer sentiment were less pessimistic regarding the economic situation of Bulgaria for the next 12 months (-21 vs -26.3) and the financial situation of their household (-7.7 vs -12.8).

At the same time, assessments on saving propensity were slightly more positive a year from now (-48 vs -50.8).

Meanwhile, households were more unlikely to incur major purchases for durable goods (-21.5 vs -20.6) and concern was amplified for unemployment for the next twelve months (19.5 vs 18).

Lastly, tension on inflation softened (18.8 vs 25.7).

data.title: MPC Energy Solutions N.V. completes Annual General Meeting
data.description:
Amsterdam / Oslo 9 May 2025 - The Annual General Meeting of MPC Energy Solutions N.V. (the “Company”) was held today, Friday, 9 May 2025 at 10:00 a.m. CET as a digital meeting. The Annual General Meeting adopted all valid

resolutions on the agenda. Please see the attached minutes of the meeting for details. The minutes of the meeting as well as a recording have also been published on the Company’s website: Annual General Meeting

For further information, please contact ir@mpc-energysolutions.com.

About MPC Energy Solutions

MPC Energy Solutions ("MPCES") develops, builds, owns and operates renewable energy assets in Latin America, with a focus on utility-scale solar photovoltaics (PV) and storage technologies. More details at www.mpc-energysolutions.com

data.title: BeammWave Interviewed by Redeye – Continued transition towards high volume products
data.description:
Stefan Svedberg, CEO at BeammWave, interviewed by Rasmus Jacobsson – Equity Analyst, Redeye, to reflect on the key milestones during the first quarter of 2025.

BeammWave has released its next generation ADP1 platform. Driven by customer readiness for project advancement and aligned with BeammWave's internal roadmap, the release gives a tantalising indication of customer progress.

“The market is no longer questioning whether digital beamforming works – now they’re asking when we can deliver at scale”, says Stefan Svedberg, CEO at BeammWave.

See and listen to the interview here:

Analyst Interview BeammWave-Redeye

Sign up for BeammWave's newsletter at:

BeammWave Newsletter

data.title: Greek industrial output rises 1.7% y/y in March
data.description:
Greek industrial output rose by 1.7% year-on-year in March, after an upwardly revised 6.0% increase in February, data from statistics service ELSTAT showed on Friday.

A breakdown of index components showed manufacturing production rose 1.9% from the same month in 2024. Electricity output was up by 0.6% year-on-year, and mining production was up 1.5%.

Compared to the same month a year earlier, in March the greatest percentage increase was recorded in the mining of coal and lignite sector, while the highest percentage decrease was recorded in the wood and products of wood and cork division, ELSTAT said.

data.title: Green Landscaping Group Buys Germany-based Wagner Straßen-, Tief- & Rohrleitungsbau
data.description:
Green Landscaping Group acquired Wagner Straßen-, Tief- & Rohrleitungsbau, a provider of earthwork, civil engineering and landscaping services based in Lebus, Germany.

The cash-and-stock deal would bolster the position of the Swedish landscaping and outdoor maintenance group in the greater Berlin area, according to a Friday release.

data.title: Greek EU-harmonised inflation eases to 2.6% y/y in April
data.description:
Greece's annual EU-harmonised inflation slowed to 2.6% in April from 3.1% in March, data showed on Friday.

Month-on-month consumer inflation rose by 0.5%

The cost of food and non-alcoholic beverages rose 2.1% year-on-year in April, with prices in hotels, cafes and restaurants going up 5.9%, the data showed.

EU-harmonised inflation is an index of components that is used across the EU to measure inflation in a consistent way.

Official statistics service ELSTAT also reported that headline consumer inflation in April dropped to 2.0% from 2.4% in March.

data.title: Ethylene prices drift lower in Europe
data.description: This week, ethylene prices dropped in the European region.An industry source in Europe informed a Polymerupdate team member, "The European ethylene market has remained in a mostly stagnant state due to low trading activity. Market participants have observed that there is little momentum and that few transactions are being completed. Due to cautious purchasing practices, adequate contract allocations, and general market uncertainties, buyer interest in acquiring spot volumes has remained low. With no notable factors propelling the market in either direction, the general lack of activity reflects a weak demand environment and helps to maintain a comparatively stable pricing trend. The source added, "Despite a few unforeseen production problems, sources reported that supply seemed to be more plentiful this week. Perhaps as a result of the end-of-week holiday in Europe, which further slowed market activity, no new spot trades were reported. Additional downward pressure on domestic prices could come from anticipated US imports in May. Although the modest increase seen in some regions in April as a result of worries about the effects of EU tariffs on US polyethylene (PE) appears to have subsided, structural demand is generally characterized as stable. Although some scheduled maintenance on derivatives is currently underway, which might be impacting specific balances, the general mood is still pessimistic, the outlook for demand is unclear, and market players are taking a cautious, wait-and-see stance while opting for hand-to-mouth strategies. On Thursday, spot ethylene prices were assessed at Euro 735-745/mt FD Northwest Europe, a drop of Euro (-20/mt) from the previous week. CIF NWE ethylene prices were assessed at USD 765-775/mt, a week on week decline of USD (-25/mt).Currently, the supply of ethylene in the Mediterranean market is more constrained than in Northwest Europe (NWE), and this disparity has intensified over the past week due to unexpected outages at crackers, as reported by market sources. These interruptions have further limited the already scarce regional supply. Although the arbitrage window from the U.S. remains open, conversations regarding imports have been subdued. Market participants noted a lack of activity in this sector, despite indicative pricing for U.S. imports showing significant discounts of around 42% to 45%, indicating that logistical challenges or a lack of market confidence may be hindering interest in capitalizing on the arbitrage opportunity.The European ethylene contract price for May 2025 settled at the Euro 1135/MT FD North West Europe levels. This price represents a plunge of Euro 70/MT from its April 2025 settlement levels.

data.title: REG - Sona Asset Mgmt (UK) American Axle & Mfg. - Form 8.3 - American Axle & Mfg Hldgs, Inc.
data.description:
RNS Number : 0785I Sona Asset Management (UK) LLP 09 May 2025

1.         KEY INFORMATION

(a) Full name of discloser:

data.title: Jefferies Adjusts Nippon Shinyaku's Price Target to 3,500 Yen From 3,800 Yen, Keeps at Hold
data.description: Nippon Shinyaku has an average rating of hold and mean price target of 4,232.22 yen, according to analysts polled by FactSet.
